skill.name: fiftyone-dataset-inference
skill.description: Create FiftyOne datasets from local media directories, import common label formats, run model inference, and store prediction fields for review.
skill.summary: Build FiftyOne datasets from local media and run model inference workflows.
skill.author: AdonaiVera

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command and weak cryptography patterns, but manual review found these are mostly Markdown inline code, fenced examples, and text matches. The remaining real risk is medium because the skill legitimately scans local directories, imports local files, and may offer dependency installation commands after model failures.

medium_findings.title: Local Filesystem Access Through User-Supplied Paths
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to scan user directories with shell commands and import media from absolute paths. This is expected for dataset ingestion, but it can expose local filenames and process unintended directories if paths are not confirmed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The workflow explicitly uses ls and find on user-selected directories, then imports files from absolute paths. The risk is real but tied to the legitimate purpose of this skill.

medium_findings.title: Dependency Install Commands May Be Suggested
medium_findings.description: The skill says model failures may return an install_command and tells the agent to offer to run it. Running generated package installation commands can modify the environment, so users should approve exact commands first.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text directly references install_command and package installation. It does not force execution, but it creates a meaningful environment modification risk.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Findings Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The many external command alerts are caused by Markdown inline code and fenced examples, including plugin names, function calls, and dataset type names. No Ruby backtick execution or executable source file was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is in a Markdown skill file and appears inside inline code spans or fenced examples. There is no Ruby source or shell backtick execution syntax in an executable file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: The high blocker findings do not correspond to cryptographic code. The flagged lines contain prose or model and field names, not MD5, SHA1, DES, or other weak algorithm usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the exact lines found no cryptographic API or algorithm selection. The matches appear to be substring collisions in ordinary documentation text.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The network alerts point to FiftyOne documentation resources. They are static reference links and do not send data or execute network requests from the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All hardcoded URLs are visible documentation links under docs.voxel51.com. No request code, telemetry, or exfiltration behavior was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Filesystem Access Through User-Supplied Paths
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs the agent to scan user directories with shell commands and import media from absolute paths. This is expected for dataset ingestion, but it can expose local filenames and process unintended directories if paths are not confirmed.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The workflow explicitly uses ls and find on user-selected directories, then imports files from absolute paths. The risk is real but tied to the legitimate purpose of this skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dependency Install Commands May Be Suggested
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill says model failures may return an install_command and tells the agent to offer to run it. Running generated package installation commands can modify the environment, so users should approve exact commands first.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The text directly references install_command and package installation. It does not force execution, but it creates a meaningful environment modification risk.

content.value_statement: Computer vision teams need reliable dataset setup before model review. This skill guides local imports, label mapping, inference, and FiftyOne app validation.
actual_capabilities: Scans a local directory to identify media files and likely label formats.
Creates persistent FiftyOne datasets through the Voxel51 utility operators.
Imports media-only datasets or labeled datasets in supported formats.
Runs supported FiftyOne Zoo models and stores predictions in a chosen field.
Launches the FiftyOne app and filters views to inspect prediction results.
Provides troubleshooting guidance for common import and inference errors.
limitations: Requires a running FiftyOne MCP server and the listed Voxel51 plugins.
Depends on local file paths that the user can access from the environment.
Some model choices require extra packages or larger compute resources.
Does not train models or evaluate metrics beyond the available FiftyOne workflow.

use_cases.title: Prepare vision datasets for review
use_cases.description: Import image or video folders into FiftyOne and verify sample counts before analysis.

use_cases.title: Compare labels with model predictions
use_cases.description: Load COCO, YOLO, VOC, or classification labels and add prediction fields from a supported model.

use_cases.title: Standardize dataset onboarding
use_cases.description: Use a repeatable workflow for directory inspection, dataset naming, label fields, and app cleanup.

prompt_templates.title: Import a media folder
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect this local media directory and propose a FiftyOne dataset name before importing it.

prompt_templates.title: Import labeled data
prompt_templates.prompt: Detect the label format in this dataset folder, then prepare the correct FiftyOne import plan.

prompt_templates.title: Run model predictions
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply a fast FiftyOne Zoo detection model to this dataset and store predictions in a new field.

prompt_templates.title: Build a full review workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create the dataset, import labels, run inference, open the app, filter prediction results, and summarize validation checks.

output_examples.input: A folder contains JPEG files and one COCO annotations file.
output_examples.output: The skill reports the detected media and label format, proposes dataset settings, and asks for confirmation before import.

output_examples.input: A user wants predictions on an existing FiftyOne dataset.
output_examples.output: The skill sets dataset context, launches the app, applies a selected model, and filters samples with prediction fields.

output_examples.input: A model fails because a package is missing.
output_examples.output: The skill explains the missing dependency and asks before any installation command is run.

best_practices: Confirm dataset name, source path, label format, and label field before import.
Use separate fields for ground truth labels and model predictions.
Start with smaller models before running slower models on large datasets.
anti_patterns: Do not import a directory before reviewing the detected files with the user.
Do not reuse an existing dataset name without confirming overwrite or deletion behavior.
Do not run dependency installation commands without showing the exact command first.

faq.question: Does this skill require FiftyOne?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires a running FiftyOne MCP server and Voxel51 plugins for import, inference, and utilities.

faq.question: Can it import labels?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents imports for COCO, YOLO, VOC, CVAT, KITTI, TensorFlow, and classification directory formats.

faq.question: Can it run model inference?
faq.answer: Yes. It applies supported FiftyOne Zoo models and stores predictions in a user-selected field.

faq.question: Does it access local files?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow scans and imports local media paths after user review and confirmation.

faq.question: Will it install dependencies automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill says to offer installation when dependencies are missing, so exact commands should be approved first.

faq.question: Does it train custom models?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on dataset creation, import, FiftyOne Zoo inference, app viewing, and troubleshooting.
